prompt: ICD-10-CM codes are used primarily to report:

options.text: Diagnoses and symptoms/reasons for the encounter

explanation: ICD-10-CM classifies diseases/symptoms; CPT/HCPCS report services/procedures/supplies.

distractorExplanations.d: POS is a claim field, not diagnosis coding.

prompt: When a definitive diagnosis is established after workup, outpatient coding generally reports:

options.text: The confirmed diagnosis rather than the previously suspected rule-out alone when confirmed
Signs/symptoms instead of confirmed DX always

explanation: Outpatient rules differ from inpatient uncertain diagnosis rules; code confirmed conditions to the highest specificity supported.

distractorExplanations.a: Do not code uncertain outpatient diagnoses as if confirmed.
distractorExplanations.c: Symptoms used when no confirmed DX.

prompt: CPT Category I codes generally describe:

options.text: Emerging technologies only as Category III
Temporary national codes only as HCPCS G/Q always
Established medical services and procedures widely performed

explanation: Category I are main CPT services; Category III are emerging; HCPCS Level II covers many supplies/drugs/services.

prompt: NEC in ICD-10-CM means:

options.text: Not elsewhere classified — other specified when a more precise code is unavailable

explanation: NEC/NOS conventions guide residual code selection when documentation specificity is limited or classification lacks detail.

prompt: A combination code is used when:

options.text: A single code captures a condition and its manifestation/associated condition as directed
Two unrelated visits are merged illegally

explanation: Guidelines instruct use of combination codes when available rather than multiple codes unnecessarily.

distractorExplanations.b: Visits aren't combined via DX combo rules that way.

prompt: Sequencing the principal/first-listed diagnosis should reflect:

options.text: The code that pays the most always regardless of documentation
The main reason for the encounter/admission per guidelines
Alphabetical first code in the book

explanation: Code sequencing follows official guidelines and documentation—not reimbursement maximization schemes.

distractorExplanations.a: Upcoding/sequencing for payment only is noncompliant.

prompt: CPT guidelines and notes at section/heading levels are:

options.text: Instructional and must be followed for correct coding

explanation: Parenthetical notes, includes/excludes-type instructions, and section guidelines control code use.

prompt: External cause codes (V00–Y99 concepts) are used to:

options.text: Replace all diagnosis codes on every claim
Provide information about how an injury/poisoning occurred, when required/assigned

explanation: External causes supplement injury coding; they do not replace the injury diagnosis.

prompt: Laterality in ICD-10-CM means coding must capture:

options.text: Left, right, or bilateral when the classification provides those options and documentation supports them
Only unspecified always if paid better

explanation: Choose the most specific laterality code supported; unspecified if not documented.

distractorExplanations.b: Don't pick unspecified to manipulate payment.

prompt: What is the purpose of the Alphabetic Index and Tabular List process in ICD-10-CM?

options.text: Use only the Index and never the Tabular
Locate a candidate code in the Index, then verify and complete coding in the Tabular List
Assign CPT from the ICD Index

explanation: Never code from the Index alone; Tabular instructions control final assignment.

distractorExplanations.d: ICD Index is not for CPT.

prompt: When a definitive diagnosis has not been established for an outpatient encounter, ICD-10-CM coding should:

options.text: Always use a Z00 code only
Code signs, symptoms, or abnormal findings that are documented as the reason for the encounter
Use inpatient principal diagnosis rules exclusively

explanation: Outpatient guidelines allow coding of documented signs/symptoms when no confirmed diagnosis exists.

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: Always use a Z00 code only
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: Use inpatient principal diagnosis rules exclusively

prompt: Which code set is used primarily to report medical procedures and services by physicians?
explanation: CPT codes describe procedures/services; ICD-10-CM describes diagnoses.

distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: NDC drug package codes only

prompt: Excludes1 notes in ICD-10-CM generally mean:

options.text: The codes may always be reported together
The conditions are mutually exclusive and should not be coded together
The note can be ignored if the coder prefers
Only inpatient coders read Excludes notes

explanation: Excludes1 indicates the excluded condition should not be coded with the code (true exclude).

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: The codes may always be reported together
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: The note can be ignored if the coder prefers
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: Only inpatient coders read Excludes notes

prompt: HCPCS Level II codes are primarily used for:

options.text: Supplies, drugs, durable medical equipment, and certain services not in CPT

explanation: Level II HCPCS covers products/services outside CPT (e.g., DME, injectables).

prompt: Place of service codes on professional claims indicate:

options.text: The setting where the service was rendered

explanation: POS codes identify the service location (office, inpatient hospital, etc.).

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: The patient's home ZIP forever

prompt: Office/outpatient E/M level selection (current AMA framework) is based primarily on:

options.text: Medical decision making or total time on the date of the encounter, per guidelines
Only the number of bullet exam elements from 1995 forever exclusively

explanation: Since 2021 office E/M revisions, MDM or time drives levels; older exam-bullet counting is not the current office paradigm.

distractorExplanations.b: 1995/97 exam scoring is largely superseded for office/outpatient.

prompt: New patient vs established patient for E/M depends on whether the patient has received face-to-face professional services from the same group/specialty within:
explanation: CPT defines established as seen by same specialty/group within 3 years (with nuances).

prompt: Medical Decision Making complexity considers:

options.text: Only the font size of the note
Only how long the patient waited
Number/complexity of problems, data reviewed/analyzed, and risk of management
Whether the waiting room was full

explanation: MDM elements are problems, data, and risk under AMA E/M methodology.

prompt: Time-based office E/M coding includes:

options.text: Only the minute the provider entered the room
Time spent by the patient driving
Total time on the date of the encounter spent on the patient's care (including non-face-to-face), per CPT rules

explanation: Counseling/coordination and other activities on that date may count when using time, per definitions.

prompt: A preventive medicine E/M visit is distinct from a problem-oriented visit because it:

options.text: Focuses on routine preventive evaluation appropriate to age/risk rather than a chief complaint problem management primarily
Is reported with only anesthesia codes

explanation: Preventive visits use preventive CPT codes; significant separately identifiable problem E/M may be reported with modifier 25 when supported.

prompt: Modifier 25 indicates:

options.text: Significant, separately identifiable E/M service by the same physician on the same day as a procedure

explanation: Modifier 25 appends to the E/M, not the procedure, when documentation supports separateness.

prompt: Critical care coding requires:

options.text: High-complexity decision making for critically ill/injured patients with specific time thresholds and organ system failure risk

explanation: Critical care is a defined CPT service with time and clinical criteria—not ordinary E/M.

prompt: Consultation codes vs transfer of care: a consultation conceptually involves:

options.text: Always taking over complete care without request
Advice/opinion requested by another provider, with report back (payer policies vary widely on consult codes)

explanation: Many payers (notably Medicare historically) limit consult coding; understand request/render/report concepts and current payer rules.

distractorExplanations.a: Transfer of care differs from consult.
distractorExplanations.c: NF discharge has its own E/M.

prompt: Place of service code on a claim identifies:

options.text: Where the service was rendered (e.g., office, outpatient hospital, ED)

explanation: POS affects payment and must match the service setting.

prompt: When billing an E/M with a minor procedure the same day, documentation should show:

options.text: No history or MDM for the E/M portion ever
The E/M is significant and separately identifiable beyond the usual pre/post work of the procedure if both are billed
That the procedure was not performed
Only a cloned note from another patient

explanation: Payers scrutinize same-day E/M + procedure; modifier 25 and solid documentation are key.

distractorExplanations.c: Procedure must have been done if billed.
distractorExplanations.d: Cloning wrong-patient notes is fraud risk.

prompt: For office/outpatient E/M visits, prolonged service reporting (when applicable) generally requires:

options.text: Meeting time thresholds beyond the primary E/M level when using time-based prolonged codes
Using only the lowest E/M level

explanation: Prolonged services depend on documented total time beyond the coded E/M threshold.

distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Always coding critical care instead
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: Using only the lowest E/M level

prompt: Which element is part of medical decision making (MDM) under current office E/M guidelines?

options.text: Number of family members in the waiting room
Number and complexity of problems addressed

explanation: MDM includes problems addressed, data, and risk of management.

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: Number of family members in the waiting room
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Color of the clinic walls
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: Whether the patient arrived early

prompt: A consultation CPT service (when payer recognizes consults) typically requires:

options.text: A request for opinion/advice, rendering of services, and a written report back to the requesting source
Only a nursing phone triage note
That the consultant become the primary care physician automatically

explanation: Classic consult requirements: request, render, report (payer policies vary).

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: No request from another clinician
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Only a nursing phone triage note
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: That the consultant become the primary care physician automatically

prompt: Emergency department E/M codes are selected based primarily on:

options.text: The documented key components/MDM appropriate to ED E/M levels (not typical office time rules)
Only the length of the waiting room delay

explanation: ED E/M leveling follows ED-specific CPT rules emphasizing history/exam/MDM as applicable.

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: Whether the patient has insurance
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Only the length of the waiting room delay

prompt: When coding a problem-oriented visit during a scheduled preventive visit (same day), correct coding often involves:

options.text: Billing only the problem visit and never the preventive
Reporting both services when significant and separately identifiable, with modifier 25 on the problem E/M when appropriate

explanation: Significant separately identifiable problem E/M may be reported with preventive care using modifier 25.

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: Billing only the problem visit and never the preventive
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Using two identical preventive codes
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: Omitting diagnoses for the problem

prompt: The surgical package (global surgery) typically includes:

options.text: Local infiltration, certain related E/M, and routine postoperative care within the global period as defined

explanation: CPT surgical package and payer global periods define what is bundled vs separately reportable.

distractorExplanations.d: Post-op visits in global are often bundled.

prompt: Modifier 78 indicates:

options.text: Unplanned return to the OR for a related procedure during the postoperative period

explanation: Know 58/78/79 distinctions for returns during global periods.

prompt: NCCI edits primarily exist to:

options.text: Prevent improper reporting of services that should not be billed together

explanation: NCCI PTP edits and MUEs control unbundling; modifiers may bypass when clinically appropriate (e.g., 59/X{EPSU}).

prompt: Anesthesia coding typically uses:

options.text: Only ICD procedure codes on professional claims always
CPT anesthesia codes with base units + time units + modifying units (payer rules)

explanation: Anesthesia payment formulas combine base and time; physical status/qualifying circumstances may apply.

prompt: Modifier 51 is used for:

options.text: Multiple procedures performed at the same session by the same provider (when applicable)

explanation: Multiple procedure reductions often apply; some codes are modifier 51 exempt.

prompt: Separate procedure designation in CPT means:

options.text: It must always be billed with every surgery
The code is usually component of a larger service and not reported in addition when integral
It is never payable under any circumstance

explanation: If performed alone for a distinct reason, a 'separate procedure' may be reportable; otherwise bundled.

prompt: Wound repair coding selection depends mainly on:
explanation: Add lengths of same classification/anatomic grouping per CPT rules; intermediate includes layered closure etc.

prompt: Global surgical periods commonly used by Medicare concepts include:

options.text: 0-day, 10-day, and 90-day globals depending on the procedure

explanation: Minor procedures often 0/10; major often 90—verify each code.

prompt: Assistant surgeon modifiers (e.g., 80) indicate:

options.text: Another surgeon assists the primary surgeon when medically necessary and allowed

explanation: Documentation must support necessity; payer assistant policies vary (including PA/NP assisting modifiers).

prompt: Add-on codes in CPT are identified by:

options.text: A '+' symbol and are never reported as stand-alone primary codes

explanation: Add-on codes are reported in addition to a primary code; modifier 51 is typically not used on add-ons.

prompt: The surgical package (global surgery) typically includes:

options.text: All unrelated E/M visits for one year
The procedure and related typical pre-/intra-/postoperative care for the global period

explanation: Global surgical package covers the operation and routine related care in the global period.

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: All unrelated E/M visits for one year
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Only the anesthesia professional's time

prompt: Modifier 51 is generally used to indicate:

options.text: Multiple procedures performed at the same session by the same provider

explanation: Modifier 51 reports multiple procedures; payer edits may affect its use.

prompt: Anesthesia time for reporting typically begins when:

options.text: The surgeon makes the incision only
The anesthesiologist begins to prepare the patient for anesthesia in the OR/procedure area
The patient schedules the surgery online
The recovery room nurse discharges the patient

explanation: Anesthesia time starts with preparation for anesthesia induction and ends when the anesthesiologist is no longer in personal attendance.

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: The surgeon makes the incision only
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: The patient schedules the surgery online
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: The recovery room nurse discharges the patient

prompt: Separate procedure designation in CPT means the service is:

options.text: Always billed in addition to a more extensive related procedure
Usually considered integral to a larger procedure and not separately reported when performed as part of that larger service

explanation: “Separate procedure” codes are typically not unbundled from a more comprehensive related procedure.

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: Always billed in addition to a more extensive related procedure
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: Never payable under any circumstance

prompt: Physical status modifiers (P1–P6) in anesthesia coding describe:

options.text: The patient's ASA physical status classification
Whether the case was elective marketing

explanation: Anesthesia physical status modifiers communicate ASA classification for risk/payment policies.

distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Whether the case was elective marketing
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: The CPT surgical global days

prompt: Upcoding is:

options.text: Reporting a higher-level service than documented/supported
Reporting a lower level than performed intentionally always as the definition

explanation: Upcoding is a compliance/fraud risk; downcoding underreports services.

prompt: The False Claims Act targets:

options.text: Honest clerical corrections via timely voids/replacements
Knowingly submitting false or fraudulent claims to the government

explanation: Knowledge includes deliberate ignorance/reckless disregard in FCA jurisprudence—compliance programs matter.

prompt: HIPAA privacy rules govern:

options.text: Protected health information use and disclosure

explanation: Covered entities must safeguard PHI; minimum necessary and patient rights apply.

prompt: HCPCS Level II codes commonly report:

options.text: Drugs, supplies, DME, and certain services not in CPT

explanation: A-codes, J-codes, etc., are Level II examples used on claims with CPT as applicable.

distractorExplanations.c: Anesthesia base is CPT anesthesia methodology.

prompt: ABN (Advance Beneficiary Notice) is used when:

options.text: A provider believes Medicare may deny a service as not reasonable/necessary and the beneficiary may be liable
A service is always covered without issue
The patient is under age 1 always

explanation: ABN shifts financial liability for expected denials when properly executed for Medicare Part B situations.

distractorExplanations.c: Covered services don't need ABN for that reason.

prompt: National Provider Identifier (NPI) is:

options.text: A unique identification number for covered health care providers
A patient's SSN replacement on all public posts

explanation: NPIs identify providers on claims; do not confuse with taxonomy or PIN legacy systems.

distractorExplanations.d: SSNs must not be publicly posted.

prompt: Unbundling means:

options.text: Billing component codes separately when a comprehensive code includes them, contrary to rules

explanation: Unbundling to increase payment violates NCCI/CPT inclusive coding principles.

prompt: Medical necessity means:

options.text: Any service a patient requests for convenience only always payable
Services are reasonable and necessary for diagnosis/treatment per payer coverage criteria

explanation: Coverage policies/LCDs/NCDs define necessity; documentation must support it.

prompt: A compliance program commonly includes:

options.text: Policies, training, auditing, and a process to report/respond to issues
Destroying records after one week always

explanation: OIG seven-element concepts emphasize prevention and detection of fraud/abuse.

prompt: Modifier 59 (or X{EPSU}) is used to indicate:

options.text: A distinct procedural service not normally reported together, when criteria are met

explanation: Use distinct-service modifiers only with documentation; overuse is an audit target.

prompt: The False Claims Act primarily targets:

options.text: Knowingly submitting false or fraudulent claims for government payment

explanation: FCA liability attaches to knowing false claims to federal healthcare programs.

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: Late employee time cards only

prompt: Upcoding means:

options.text: Selecting a code that accurately reflects documentation
Reporting a higher-level service than supported by documentation to increase payment

explanation: Upcoding is a compliance risk and potential fraud when unsupported.

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: Selecting a code that accurately reflects documentation
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Using ICD-10-CM seventh characters correctly

prompt: NCCI edits are designed to:

options.text: Prevent improper payment of services that should not be billed together

explanation: National Correct Coding Initiative edits promote correct coding and avoid unbundling.

distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: Assign DRGs for physician claims

prompt: An Advance Beneficiary Notice (ABN) is used in Medicare contexts primarily to:

options.text: Notify a beneficiary that Medicare may deny a service so they can accept financial responsibility
Authorize a research study without consent

explanation: ABNs shift potential financial liability for anticipated Medicare noncoverage when properly used.

distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: Authorize a research study without consent

prompt: Medical necessity for coding/billing means:

options.text: The service is convenient for the clinic schedule
The service is reasonable and necessary for diagnosis or treatment per payer/coverage rules and documentation
Any requested service must be paid

explanation: Coverage depends on documented medical necessity aligned with payer policies.

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: The service is convenient for the clinic schedule
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Any requested service must be paid
